title: helianthemum guttatum subsp. inconspicuum
intro: Helianthemum guttatum subsp. inconspicuum is a subspecies of the rockrose plant, belonging to the Cistaceae family, and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Helianthemum guttatum subsp. inconspicuum include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its flowers and leaves in water or oil, and it is also used in salves and creams to promote wound healing and skin health.
